title: ammophila arenaria proles arundinacea
intro: Ammophila arenaria proles arundinacea, also known as Marram grass, is a coastal grass species native to the British Isles and other parts of Europe. It has been used in traditional herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant qualities. The health benefits of Marram grass include the ability to soothe respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing. Marram grass can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and has also been used in traditional folk remedies for its antiseptic and expectorant properties.
